latestPosts.title: AI Infrastructure on AWS: SageMaker, EKS GPU Scheduling, and Cost-Efficient Inference
latestPosts.description: Production AI/ML infrastructure on AWS: SageMaker real-time vs async inference endpoints, EKS GPU scheduling with nvidia device plugin, EC2 GPU instance selection (p4, g5, inf2 Inferentia), Spot instances for training workloads, and the architecture decisions that keep GPU bills under control.
latestPosts.excerpt: Running AI workloads in production is a different discipline from running web services. The hardware is expensive, the failure modes are opaque, and the cost curve compounds fast. After operating inference infrastructure…

latestPosts.title: Idempotency Keys in APIs: Retries, Duplicate Requests, and Exactly-Once Illusions
latestPosts.description: A production guide to designing idempotent APIs with idempotency keys, request fingerprinting, response replay, database constraints, TTL cleanup, and race-condition handling.
latestPosts.excerpt: Any API that changes state will eventually receive the same request more than once. Mobile clients retry on flaky networks. Load balancers retry after connection resets. Users double-click. Workers crash after doing the …

latestPosts.title: Cache Invalidation Patterns: TTL, Write-Through, Cache-Aside, and Event-Driven Eviction
latestPosts.description: A practical guide to cache invalidation in production systems: TTLs, cache-aside, write-through, versioned keys, event-driven eviction, stampede prevention, and stale-read tradeoffs.
latestPosts.excerpt: Cache invalidation is hard because the cache is not the source of truth. It is a copy, and every copy has a consistency problem. The real question is not "how do we make cache perfectly consistent?" The better question i…

latestPosts.title: Database Connection Pool Tuning: HikariCP, PostgreSQL, and Traffic Spikes
latestPosts.description: A practical guide to tuning database connection pools for Spring Boot services: pool sizing, HikariCP settings, PostgreSQL limits, slow query impact, metrics, and production failure modes.
latestPosts.excerpt: Database connection pools look boring until they take production down. Most backend services do not fail because the database has zero capacity. They fail because the application opens too many connections, holds them to…

latestPosts.title: Kafka Consumer Lag: Causes, Diagnosis, and Production Fixes
latestPosts.description: A practical Kafka consumer lag playbook covering partition skew, slow processing, rebalances, max.poll settings, poison messages, autoscaling, metrics, and safe recovery strategies.
latestPosts.excerpt: Kafka consumer lag is not a root cause. It is a symptom. Lag means consumers are not keeping up with producers for one or more partitions. The fix depends on why: slow processing, too few consumers, partition skew, rebal…
